name: Space Complexity
description: Questions on auxiliary space and memory usage.

questions.question: Space complexity measures:
questions.explanation: Space complexity usually refers to extra space (auxiliary) beyond the input.

questions.question: Creating a copy of a list of n elements uses:
questions.explanation: A full copy requires n additional slots, so O(n) space.

questions.question: Recursion without tail-call optimization uses space proportional to:
questions.explanation: Each recursive call adds a stack frame; depth equals recursion depth.

questions.question: Building a set from n elements uses at least:
questions.explanation: You must store up to n distinct elements, so Omega(n) space.

questions.question: In-place sorting (e.g. list.sort()) uses:
questions.explanation: Timsort and similar in-place sorts use O(1) or small constant extra space.
